author: lia
content: Hey @marcus @dana - I know you're both hearing 'P0' from different directions right now. I'm seeing the overlap in the subsystem work. I'm going to pull some data on the revenue risk vs. incident cost so I can get our Director to make a call on the sequence. Let's keep the focus on the patch for today while I clear the air.

author: lia
content: I'm worried that if I don't force a trade-off conversation now, the team will just 'hero' their way into a meltdown. I need to frame this to the Director as a choice between two types of churn.

author: marcus
content: @lia Glad you're on it. Just so we're clear: if we try to layer the upsell on top of the current state of that subsystem before D1 is fully deployed, we aren't just looking at revenue risk—we're looking at a total blackout. I'm focusing 100% on the patch.

author: dana
content: @lia @marcus I agree. The upsell code (D2) touches the exact same logic that's currently failing in prod. If I push my changes before the patch is stable, I'm effectively building on quicksand. I'm hitting pause on the upsell logic today to help Marcus verify the fix.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Copy that. 'Building on quicksand' is the perfect framing. I'm taking that specific risk—total blackout vs. delayed upsell—to the Director now. Stay focused on D1; I'll handle the heat from Sales.

author: lia
content: I'm pushing for O1. I've realized that if I don't name the 'total blackout' risk, Leadership will just see two green bars and keep pushing. I'm forcing the 'sequence' conversation today.

author: marcus
content: @lia Thanks. If the Director asks for a 'middle ground,' tell them there isn't one. It's either a stable foundation or a crater. @dana, I've pushed the first set of guardrails to staging—could use your eyes on the concurrency logic.

author: dana
content: @marcus On it. Checking the concurrency logic now. If we don't get these guardrails right, the upsell won't just be delayed, it'll be impossible to scale anyway. @lia let us know the second you hear back; I'm keeping the D2 branch cold until we have a written pivot.

author: lia
content: I've sent the meeting invite to the Director for the O1 reset. I'm framing it as: 'Choose which risk you want to accept: a delayed upsell or a total platform blackout.' No middle ground.

author: lia
content: Update: Director meeting is set for this afternoon. I've used your 'quicksand' and 'crater' feedback directly in the brief. Keep the focus on D1; I'm not letting them mark D2 as 'active' until we get the green light to sequence it *after* the fix.

author: marcus
content: @lia Appreciate you taking the heat. If they push back, remind them that 'parallelizing' these two items is just a slower way to fail both. @dana, concurrency logic is looking better after your notes—let's get this patch to a 'done' state by EOD so Lia has a win to show them.

author: dana
content: @marcus Agreed. Finalizing the last check on the guardrails now. If we ship this today, @lia has the strongest possible leverage to show we're clearing the path for the upsell, not just stalling.

author: lia
content: Heading into the O1 meeting. I'm bringing the 'crater' analogy. If I can't get a signed-off ranked list today, I'll have failed to protect their focus.

author: lia
content: Meeting starts in 5 minutes. You two getting D1 to the finish line is the best ammo I have. I'm going in to make D1 the *only* official priority until it's signed off. Stand by.

author: marcus
content: @lia Good luck. D1 is passing all tests in staging. We're hitting 'deploy' now. You can tell them the 'bleeding' is officially stopped, so now we just need the space to keep it that way.

author: dana
content: @lia Go get 'em. D1 is officially in prod and looks stable. We've proven we can fix the foundation; now we just need the Director to agree that we don't start the upsell (D2) until we're sure the foundation won't crack again.

author: lia
content: Result: Director has officially signed off on sequencing. D1 is the priority; D2 stays in the backlog until we've had a week of zero incidents. We have the air cover we needed. Great work on the patch, everyone.

author: lia
content: O1 is complete. I got the ranked list in writing. The 'crater' analogy worked—the Director realized that an upsell on a broken system is just a refund waiting to happen.

author: marcus
content: @lia That is a huge win. Having that week of stability as a buffer is exactly what we needed to avoid a repeat of this mess. @dana, let's use some of this 'quiet' time to prep the D2 architecture so it's actually robust when we flip the switch.

author: dana
content: @lia Huge result. Thanks for getting that in writing—it makes a world of difference. @marcus, agreed. I'll start drafting the D2 arch docs today so we can review them without the 'prod is on fire' stress hanging over us.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Now that we have the air cover, I want to make sure we learn from this. I've scheduled a retro for tomorrow. No blame—just want to nail down a team norm for how we flag these 'impossible' overlaps to leadership earlier next time.

author: lia
content: O1 is done and the team is breathing easier. Moving to O2 now. I need to make sure the 'hero' culture doesn't become the default; the retro will focus on a specific norm for capacity signaling.

author: marcus
content: @lia I'm in. It'll be good to get those norms down while the 'before' state is still fresh in our minds. Thanks for fighting that battle for us.

author: dana
content: @lia Count me in for the retro. I've moved D2 back to the backlog for now to keep our board honest with the new plan. Looking forward to solidifying those norms so we don't have to play 'hero' next time.

author: lia
content: Retro is live. I've posted the agenda: 1. What triggered the 'double commitment'? 2. How do we signal 'red' capacity before the stress hits? Our goal is one concrete norm we can hold each other to.

author: lia
content: Finalizing O2 now. The team is engaged. I'm proposing a 'Capacity Redline' norm: if two 'Priority 1s' hit the board, we automatically pause work until a trade-off is signed off. No more silent heroics.

author: marcus
content: For the norm, I suggest: 'Any new P1 request during an active incident automatically triggers a 15-minute sync with the Director to drop a specific item.' No more 'just fit it in' talk.

author: dana
content: @marcus +1 to that norm. I'd also add: 'Engineering capacity is a zero-sum game.' If something is added, something must be moved to the backlog immediately on the board. It stops the 'passive-aggressive yes' from happening.
